title: calamagrostis epigejos subsp. acrathera
intro: Calamagrostis epigejos subsp. acrathera, also known as a type of fescue grass, is a perennial plant species native to Europe and parts of Asia. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving respiratory issues, and aiding in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant's roots and rhizomes are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
